description: Decentralized ClassJ+Triage adjudication with dual-quorum veto. Phase 109C: N=5 emulator nodes (testnet, code-before-operators). Real ioSwarm nodes use independent hardware stacks in production.

input_schema.triage_patterns: str|null — colon-sep triage pattern string from Phase 91

output_schema.classj_agreement_ratio: float — fraction of nodes agreeing on verdict
output_schema.triage_agreement_ratio: float — fraction of nodes agreeing on verdict
output_schema.dual_veto: bool — True when BOTH classj==BLOCK AND triage==BLOCK
output_schema.dual_veto_score: float — score override applied when dual_veto==True
output_schema.node_count: int — number of emulator nodes evaluated

quorum_config.note: 0.67 = enforcement standard (NOT 0.60 renewal standard). Tie resolution HOLD prevents false positives on split verdicts.

dual_veto_config.rationale: W2 novel — exclusive to VAPI. When ClassJ AND Triage both independently reach BLOCK (67% node agreement each), the joint false-positive probability drops multiplicatively. Score clamped to 0.80 > epistemic threshold 0.60 → always drives BLOCK verdict. Gives operators mathematical confidence to flip dry_run=False.

vhp_authorization_gate.description: Operator device must hold valid VHP to submit adjudication tasks

fail_open_direction.note: OPPOSITE of VHPRenewal fail-open (which returns approved=True). Adjudication errors lean toward CLEAR to prevent false bans.

w1_security_boundary.testnet_note: All 5 emulator nodes share same seed/logic — testnet only (code-before-operators, MockGSRGrip precedent). Evasion of one node evades all nodes simultaneously. This is acceptable for testnet; production ioSwarm nodes use independent hardware stacks (architectural security property, not code-level).
